mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, including those used in construction, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing units sold are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, including those used in construction, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the consumer price index signals a strengthening supply chain, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
